title: genista depressa subsp. csikii
intro: Genista depressa subsp. csikii, also known as Hungarian burnet, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Hungary, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its alkaloid content. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as relieving arthritis and other inflammatory conditions, as well as being used as a diuretic and for treating skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the flowers and leaves of Genista depressa subsp. csikii are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
